data.title: The Second Law is not bedrock
data.summary:
The measurable world is not the explanatory floor. What science often treats as “the Second” is only a local, visible bout of a deeper Unity–Disunity cycle. Zeroth begins with gradient; First with admissible coherence; PoLA with lower-friction path selection; Second with measurable manifestation.
Zeroth Law:  No change without gradient.
First Law Only some gradient relations can sustain coherence or admissible persistence.
PoLA - Principle of Least Action Among admissible paths, lower-friction or lower-action trajectories are selected.
Second Law A realized local bout of the Unity–Disunity cycle: a measurable episode of coherence, destabilization, transition, and reformation.
Claim: The Second is not the bedrock. It is an appearance layer of the cycle, not the ultimate explanatory floor.
Implication: Much of science studies the Second as if it were foundational, while it may be only one local manifestation of deeper gradient logic.
